name: Canggu - Echo Beach (峇里島 - 回音灘 / Pantai Batu Mejan / Echo Beach)
distance_from_dps_airport_km: 22 km (approx. 70-90 min by car depending on traffic)
distance_from_canggu_uluwatu_min: From Canggu (Batu Bolong center): 5-10 min by scooter; From Uluwatu: 70-100 min by scooter
wave_type: Reef break - predominantly a powerful left-hander (Echo Left), with a less famous right (Echo Right); short, punchy, hollow / barreling on bigger swells
skill_level: Intermediate to Advanced (Pro on bigger days). Not suitable for beginners.
wave_height_range: 3-8 ft (best 4-8 ft / head-high to double-overhead; works up to ~10 ft)
best_season: Dry season May-October is prime (consistent SW swell + offshore SE/E trade winds). Shoulder seasons (April, November) also good. Wet season (Dec-Feb) often onshore and blown out.
best_tide: Mid to high tide (incoming mid is the sweet spot). Low tide closes the wave out and exposes dangerous reef.
best_wind: Offshore from East / Southeast / Northeast (E-SE trade winds optimal); turns onshore around 10-11am
best_swell_direction: SW (Southwest) primary; also S and WSW
swell_consistency_rating: 5/5 (highly consistent, works on most swells that hit Canggu)
wave_length_meters: 30-80 m typical ride (short, fast, sectiony rather than long peeling) [uncertain]
wave_speed_rating: Fast (medium-fast on small days, fast and punchy with steep takeoff on bigger days; rewards shortboards and step-ups)
takeoff_zone_difficulty: Difficult - steep, ledgy takeoff over shallow reef; wave jacks up quickly as it hits the reef shelf. Drop-knee priority and quick paddling required. Mistimed takeoff puts you onto exposed reef.
secondary_peaks: Echo Left (main A-frame leans heavily to the left), Echo Right (shorter, less consistent right shoulder), and Echo Reef / River Mouth peaks further north toward Pererenan. An inside reform can be ridden on smaller days.
peak_crowd_level: 5/5 - take-off zone is tight and extremely crowded when good; lots of hassling, drop-ins, and paddle-battles. Peak crowd 7am-11am dry season.
access: Walk-in from the end of Jalan Pura Batu Mejan (Echo Beach road); park at La Brisa / The Lawn / Echo Beach parking area then walk down to the sand. No stairs or cave entry.
paddle_out_method: Paddle out via the channel between Echo Left peak and the Sandbar in front of La Brisa. A helpful rip-current pulls you straight to the lineup past the Sandbar, making the paddle relatively easy when timed correctly. Stay clear of breaking sets on the inside reef.
entry_fee: No beach entry fee. Parking: ~IDR 5,000 for car, IDR 2,000-3,000 for scooter at official lots.
recommended_session_time: Dawn patrol (5:30-7:30am) for glassy offshores and lighter crowd; second session at sunset if wind drops.
best_time_of_day: Before 7am to avoid both crowd and onshore wind; mid-week early morning best.
nearby_warung: La Brisa (cliffside cafe/bar), The Lawn, Sand Bar, Echo Beach House, Beach House, Mocca Cafe, Old Man's nearby; coconut and warung stalls along the access road.
alternative_spot_within_10min: Berawa (5-10 min south, mellower beach break on big-Echo days), Pererenan (5 min north - heavier reef alternative when Echo crowded), Batu Bolong (5 min south for smaller/softer waves), Old Man's (5 min south).
hazards: Shallow, sharp coral/lava reef; crowd-related collisions; strong rip current on the paddle-out channel; sharp sections of exposed reef at low tide; sea urchins on reef; fast-breaking ledge causes wipeouts onto reef.
marine_hazards: Sea urchins (urchin spines on reef - common; treat with vinegar/tweezers). Reef-fish lurking in crevices. Rare jellyfish in wet season. No significant shark concern. Bacterial water issues post-rain (Yeh Poh river runoff just north).
reef_cut_severity_index: Medium to High - reef is sharper and shallower than Batu Bolong, with exposed lava-coral chunks. Reef cuts are common when wiping out at low tide; can be deep enough to require sutures. Carry reef-cut first aid (iodine, vinegar).
rip_current_pattern: Channel rip immediately south of Echo Left peak (between peak and Sandbar) - useful for paddling out but pulls strongly on outgoing tide. Side-sweeping current along the reef can drag inexperienced surfers onto inside reef. Stronger on bigger swells.
lineup_etiquette: Strict priority on the peak; locals and regulars dominate the inside takeoff. Wait your turn in rotation; don't paddle straight to the peak. No snaking; respect priority and back-paddling will earn aggressive responses. Apologize quickly if you blow a wave.
localism_intensity: Medium - more localized than Batu Bolong; regular crew of Indonesian and expat surfers known to enforce priority. Generally not aggressive if you respect rotation and surf at your level, but vibe can turn cold if you drop in repeatedly.
respect_protocol: Don't paddle inside locals; nod and greet regulars; if you're not at intermediate-advanced level, surf Batu Bolong instead. Avoid bringing surf-school groups into the lineup. Cover up walking through warungs and temple zones.
resident_local_legends: Canggu pro/free-surf crew shred Echo regularly (e.g., Garut Widiarta, Made Lana, Diah Rahayu, Raditya Rondi have surfed here). International pros (Mason Ho, Bruce Irons) occasionally visit. [uncertain]
religious_sensitivity: Pura Batu Mejan (Hindu temple) sits directly behind the beach on the cliff - it is an active worship site, do not climb on offerings or temple structures. Observe Nyepi (Day of Silence in March) - no surfing, beach closed. Women on menstruation traditionally avoid temple grounds.
medical_nearby: Penta Medical Canggu (24h clinic on Jl. Raya Canggu), Canggu Medical Clinic, BIMC Hospital Nusa Dua (major trauma), Siloam Hospital Denpasar. Reef cuts are common here so most clinics know how to handle them quickly.
nearest_clinic_distance_km: Penta Medical Canggu ~2-3 km; BIMC Nusa Dua ~27 km; Siloam Denpasar ~17 km
rescue_jet_ski_available: No dedicated rescue jet ski stationed at Echo; Balawista lifeguards informally cover the area on busy days; instructors at adjacent surf schools provide informal rescues.
emergency_contact: Bali Emergency: 112; Balawista lifeguards: +62 361 755660; Police: 110; Ambulance: 118
nearest_surf_school: Most surf schools operate from neighboring Batu Bolong (Dian Surf School, Surf Well Bali, Sea N Surf, Odyssey, Pro Surf). Few schools teach beginners at Echo due to reef hazards; intermediate guiding only.
board_rental_availability: No on-beach rental at Echo. Rental shops on the road (Boardriders Bali Echo Beach, Drifter, Deus Ex Machina) offer shortboards and performance boards.
surf_photographer_available: Yes - regular land and water photographers shoot the Echo Left peak from the rocks and beach; photos sold via Instagram/QR code same day.
photographer_recommendation: Local Canggu photographers (e.g., @echobeachphoto-style operators, freelancers via @balisurfphotos); typical price IDR 200,000-400,000 for an edited photo pack. [uncertain]
accommodation_options: Echo Beach House, Mocca Bali, Pondok Nyoman, Hotel Tugu Bali (luxury heritage), surf camps in Canggu/Pererenan (Mondo Surf Village, Padang Padang Surf Camp Canggu). Wide range from hostels to villas within 5-15 min.
webcam_url: https://www.surfline.com/surf-report/canggu/5842041f4e65fad6a7708d40 (Surfline Canggu cam covers Echo area); https://balicams.com/camera/rivermouth (River Mouth / Echo-adjacent cam)
drone_allowed: Generally tolerated for surf photography; requires DGCA registration for >250g. Avoid flying over the temple, low over the crowded lineup, or in restricted hours. Many photographers fly drones here daily.
coworking_nearby: Yes - Dojo Bali (originally near Echo, now main branch nearby), Outpost Canggu, B Work Bali (Nelayan), Tropical Nomad; all within 5-15 min scooter.
avg_lesson_price: IDR 500,000-700,000 (~USD 32-45 / TWD 1,000-1,400) for an intermediate guiding/coaching session (most schools won't teach beginners here)
board_rental_daily: Performance shortboard IDR 150,000-200,000/day (premium); standard board IDR 100,000-150,000/day from nearby shops
water_quality_rating: Dry season (May-Oct): Fair to Good. Wet season (Nov-Mar): Poor after heavy rain due to Yeh Poh river-mouth runoff and drainage; ear/skin infection risk. Avoid surfing 24-48h after heavy rain.
annual_competition_window: Hosts occasional regional shortboard contests and free-surf sessions; Deus Sunday-style events occasionally land here. No major WSL CT event. [uncertain]
community_events: Sunset DJ sessions at La Brisa, Echo Beach House parties, beach clean-ups by Trash Hero Canggu and 4Ocean, Deus 9ft & Single Festival activities nearby, full moon events on the beach.
